Instructions for setting up prerequisites, building, and testing JerryScript on Ubuntu 14.04+.

# Purpose
This document provides instructions for setting up the development environment and building the JerryScript engine on Ubuntu 14.04+ systems. It specifies the necessary software dependencies, including compilers and tools like `gcc`, `cmake`, `bash`, `cppcheck`, `vera++`, and `python`, and provides a command to install them using `apt-get`. The document outlines various build configurations for JerryScript, such as building debug versions, adding custom CMake arguments, setting profile modes, and selecting different libc implementations. It also includes instructions for cross-compiling to specific platforms, like Raspberry Pi 2, using toolchain files. Additionally, the document describes how to run different types of tests, such as build option tests, unittests, and code checks, using the `run-tests.py` script, and provides commands to list available build and test options.
# Content Summary
This document provides instructions for setting up the development environment and building the JerryScript project. It specifies that Ubuntu 14.04+ is the supported development environment. Developers must manually install several dependencies, including a C99-compliant compiler like `gcc`, `cmake` version 2.8.12.2 or higher, and additional tools such as `bash`, `cppcheck`, `vera++`, and `python` version 2.7.6 or higher. Essential shell utilities like `awk`, `bc`, `find`, and `sed` are also required.

The document outlines the process for building JerryScript, including commands to build a debug version for Linux, with or without Link Time Optimization (LTO). It provides instructions for adding custom CMake arguments, setting profile modes (full or minimal), and selecting different libc options (jerry-libc, compiler-default, or external libc). The document also explains how to use toolchain files for cross-compilation, such as for Raspberry Pi 2.

For testing, the document describes how to check patches and run various types of tests, including build option tests, unittests, jerry-tests, jerry-test-suite, signed-off checks, cppcheck, and vera checks. It provides commands for each test type and instructions to list all available build and test options.
